perturbation.original_text: 3.1.7 The Recipient shall only and exclusively provide access to the Confidential Information to the Authorised Third Parties which: (i) are duly informed of the commercial in confidence nature of such information; and (ii) observe confidentiality obligations equivalent to and equally onerous as all the confidentiality obligations stemming from this Agreement. 3.1.8 The Recipient shall inform the GSA of the disclosure of Confidential Information to Authorised Third Parties and provide the GSA with an electronic copy of the signed confidentiality agreement between the Recipient and the Authorised Third Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.1.7 The Recipient shall only and exclusively provide access to the Confidential Information to the Authorised Third Parties which: (i) are duly informed of the commercial in confidence nature of such information. 3.1.8 The Recipient shall inform the GSA of the disclosure of Confidential Information to Authorised Third Parties.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause '(ii) observe confidentiality obligations equivalent to and equally onerous as all the confidentiality obligations stemming from this Agreement; and' from 3.1.7 we create a contradiction. It weakens the protective measures for confidential information when shared with Authorised Third Parties. While 3.1.8 still mandates informing the GSA of disclosures, the absence of a requirement for equivalent confidentiality obligations introduces a contradiction. The agreement suggests sharing confidential information, but removes the legal obligation on the third party to protect it to the same extent as the Recipient. This diminishes the overall protection intended by the agreement, which creates a uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1 This Agreement sets f o r t h t h e R e c i p i e n t ’ s obligations regarding access to, use, distribution, disclosure a n d p r o t e c t i o n o f t h e C o n f i d e n t i a l I n f o r m a t i o n provided by the GSA for the Purpose.
perturbation.justification: The original text in 3.1.7 ensures that Authorised Third Parties observe confidentiality obligations equivalent to those of the Recipient. The changed text removes this requirement, weakening the protection of Confidential Information when shared with third parties. This creates a contradiction with section 2.1, which states that the agreement sets forth the Recipient's obligations regarding the protection of Confidential Information. By removing the requirement for equivalent confidentiality obligations from third parties, the changed text diminishes the overall protection of the information, directly contradicting the intent and stated obligations in section 2.1. As per the definition of 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction', this creates a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Article 5 – Return of Confidential Information 5.1 If the Recipient fails to submit an offer, he must then return all documents provided by the GSA within two months following the deadline for the submission of offers. 5.2 If the Recipient submits an offer but is not awarded the contract, he must return all documents provided by the GSA within two months following publication of the award notice. 5.3 The Recipient shall use all reasonable endeavours to ensure that any Authorised Third Parties to whom the Recipient has supplied any Confidential Information return such Confidential Information and any copies made of them to the GSA within the applicable deadlines stipulated under Articles 5.1 and 5.2 above.
perturbation.changed_text: Article 5 – Return of Confidential Information 5.1 If the Recipient fails to submit an offer, he must then return all documents provided by the GSA within two months following the deadline for the submission of offers. 5.2 If the Recipient submits an offer but is not awarded the contract, he must return all documents provided by the GSA within two months following publication of the award notice. 5.3 The Recipient shall use all reasonable endeavours to ensure that any Authorised Third Parties to whom the Recipient has supplied any Confidential Information return such Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the final part of section 5.3 'and any copies made of them to the GSA within the applicable deadlines stipulated under Articles 5.1 and 5.2 above.', we create a contradiction. The Recipient must ensure any Authorised Third Parties return confidential information, but there's no longer a specified requirement for returning copies or adherence to deadlines. This creates a contradiction because the original intent of Article 5 was a complete and timely return of all confidential information (including copies) to maintain control and security. By removing the copy return, it creates a ambiguity regarding which information the Recipient and Third Parties must return. The main article is asking for information but the clause of the original article mentions a deadline, this creates the in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Article 5 – Return of Confidential Information 5.1 If the Recipient fails to submit an offer, he must then return all documents provided by the GSA within two months following the deadline for the submission of offers. 5.2 If the Recipient submits an offer but is not awarded the contract, he must return all documents provided by the GSA within two months following publication of the award notice.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Article 5.3 omits the requirement for Authorized Third Parties to return 'any copies made of them to the GSA within the applicable deadlines stipulated under Articles 5.1 and 5.2 above.' The contradicted text, Article 5.1 and 5.2, sets specific deadlines for returning 'all documents'. This creates a contradiction because the original intention of Article 5 was a complete and timely return of all confidential information (including copies) to maintain control and security, and by removing the copy return and deadline requirement, it is unclear if the original intent is being followed. Therefore, it does create a direct conflict within the document, because the third parties would not be required to return the copy within the same timeframe as the Recipient in 5.1 and 5.2.

perturbation.original_text: Article 7 – Duration of this Agreement and protection of the Confidential Information 7.1 This Agreement shall enter into force on the date of the last signature by the Parties and shall remain in effect until the Completion of the Purpose. 7.2 The obligations imposed by Articles 2 and 3 above with respect to the access to, use, distribution, disclosure and protection of Confidential Information for pursuing the Purpose shall apply:  for ten years with regards to Confidential Information;  in accordance with the procedures and duration specified by the authorities concerned with regards to Classified Confidential Information from the date of receipt of the Confidential Information by the Recipient, notwithstanding the Completion of the Purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: Article 7 – Duration of this Agreement and protection of the Confidential Information 7.1 This Agreement shall enter into force on the date of the last signature by the Parties and shall remain in effect until the Completion of the Purpose. 7.2 The obligations imposed by Articles 2 and 3 above with respect to the access to, use, distribution, disclosure and protection of Confidential Information for pursuing the Purpose shall apply:  in accordance with the procedures and duration specified by the authorities concerned with regards to Classified Confidential Information from the date of receipt of the Confidential Information by the Recipient, notwithstanding the Completion of the Purpose.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause 'for ten years with regards to Confidential Information', we create a contradiction within Article 7. Article 7.2 states the obligations from Articles 2 and 3 apply for ten years for regular Confidential Information, but then modifies the duration for Classified Confidential Information. By removing the 'ten years' requirement, the agreement lacks a clear duration for the protection of non-classified Confidential Information. Since Article 7 has duration it then creates the in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Article 7 – Duration of this Agreement and protection of the Confidential Information 7.1 This Agreement shall enter into force on the date of the last signature by the Parties and shall remain in effect until the Completion of the Purpose.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a duration of 'ten years with regards to Confidential Information' for the obligations imposed by Articles 2 and 3. The changed text removes this clause, creating a contradiction because the agreement no longer clearly defines the duration of protection for non-classified Confidential Information, as highlighted in the perturbation explanation. This contradicts Article 7.1 which implies that the entire agreement remains in effect until the Completion of the Purpose but Article 7.2 modifies this timeline depending on the type of information. The original text had a defined time for regular confidential information, while the changed text does not.

perturbation.original_text: 11.3 This Agreement represents the entire understanding and agreement of the Parties with respect to the Confidential Information, as defined in Article 2 above, and supersedes and cancels any and all previous declarations, negotiations, commitments, communications either oral or written, approvals, agreements and confidentiality agreements between the Parties in respect thereto. Any rights and obligations which, by their nature, are to remain in effect beyond expiration or termination of such confidentiality agreements will survive.
perturbation.changed_text: 11.3 This Agreement represents the entire understanding and agreement of the Parties with respect to the Confidential Information, and supersedes and cancels any and all previous declarations, negotiations, commitments, communications either oral or written, approvals, agreements and confidentiality agreements between the Parties in respect thereto. Any rights and obligations which, by their nature, are to remain in effect beyond expiration or termination of such confidentiality agreements will survive.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'as defined in Article 2 above' from Section 11.3, we introduce a contradiction. Article 2 describes the scope of the agreement and refers to the term "Confidential Information". By removing this clause we generate an in-text contradiction because Article 1 defines what "Confidential Information" is. If there is no article 2 definition, there is ambiguity that generates an uncertainty to this very definition that could be mentioned somewhere else in this document. In this case creating the in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11.3 This Agreement represents the entire understanding and agreement of the Parties with respect to the Confidential Information, and supersedes and cancels any and all previous declarations, negotiations, commitments, communications either oral or written, approvals, agreements and confidentiality agreements between the Parties in respect thereto. Any rights and obligations which, by their nature, are to remain in effect beyond expiration or termination of such confidentiality agreements will survive.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Confidential Information' with explicit reference to Article 2. The changed text removes 'as defined in Article 2 above'. According to the explanation provided, this omission creates ambiguity and uncertainty because Article 1 defines 'Confidential Information', and the removal of the Article 2 reference contradicts the definition located in Article 1, causing an in-text contradiction between Section 11.3 and potentially Article 1 or other sections relying on a clear definition of 'Confidential Information'.
